SEARCH
Package com.unimib.assignment3.POJO

Class Task

java.lang.Object
com.unimib.assignment3.POJO.Task
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
@Entity(name="task") public class Task extends Object implements Serializable
Task entity class.

Represents an activity with a unique identifier, a state, a time range, an associated team, and a list of assigned employees.

See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • Task

      protected Task()
      Default constructor. Initializes the task state STARTED.

    • Task

      public Task(TaskState taskState)
      Constructor with task state.
      Parameters:
      taskState - the state to assign (defaults to STARTED if null)

    • Task

      public Task(List<Employee> assignedEmployees, TaskState taskState, LocalDate startDate, LocalDate endDate)
      Constructor with employees, state, and dates.
      Parameters:
      assignedEmployees - the list of employees to assign
      taskState - the state of the task
      startDate - the start date
      endDate - the end date

  • Method Details

    • getTaskId

      public Long getTaskId()
      Getter for task ID.
      Returns:
      the task ID

    • getAssignedEmployees

      public List<Employee> getAssignedEmployees()
      Getter for assigned employees. Returns a copy of the list to maintain encapsulation.
      Returns:
      a new list containing assigned employees

    • setAssignedEmployees

      public void setAssignedEmployees(List<Employee> dipendentiAssegnati)
      Setter for assigned employees.
      Parameters:
      dipendentiAssegnati - the list of employees to set

    • getTaskState

      public TaskState getTaskState()
      Getter for task state.
      Returns:
      the current task state

    • setTaskState

      public void setTaskState(TaskState taskState)
      Setter for task state.
      Parameters:
      taskState - the task state to set

    • assignEmployee

      public void assignEmployee(Employee employee)
      Assigns an employee to the task. Manages the bidirectional relationship by adding the task to the employee's list.
      Parameters:
      employee - the employee to add

    • removeEmployee

      public void removeEmployee(Employee employee)
      Removes an employee from the task. Manages the bidirectional relationship by removing the task from the employee's list.
      Parameters:
      employee - the employee to remove

    • hasEmployee

      public boolean hasEmployee(Employee employee)
      Checks if an employee is assigned to this task.
      Parameters:
      employee - the employee to check
      Returns:
      true if present, false otherwise

    • getStartDate

      public LocalDate getStartDate()
      Getter for start date.
      Returns:
      the start date

    • setStartDate

      public void setStartDate(LocalDate startDate)
      Setter for start date. Validates that the start date is not after the end date.
      Parameters:
      startDate - the start date to set
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if start date is after end date

    • getEndDate

      public LocalDate getEndDate()
      Getter for end date.
      Returns:
      the end date

    • setEndDate

      public void setEndDate(LocalDate endDate)
      Setter for end date. Validates that the end date is not before the start date.
      Parameters:
      endDate - the end date to set
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if end date is before start date

    • getAllEmployeesId

      public List<Long> getAllEmployeesId()
      Retrieves IDs of all assigned employees.
      Returns:
      a list of employee IDs

    • getTeamTask

      public Team getTeamTask()
      Getter for the associated team.
      Returns:
      the team assigned to the task

    • setTeamTask

      public void setTeamTask(Team team)
      Setter for the associated team.
      Parameters:
      team - the team to set
